compiled_state.title: fix-bug: multi-sub-issue bugs dispatched to single investigator instead of split into separate tickets first
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

When a bug ticket contains multiple independent sub-issues (e.g., 1117-650d contains 4 CI job failures across eval tests, hook tests, shellcheck, and script tests), the fix-bug workflow dispatches a single investigation sub-agent for all sub-issues. This produces a compound investigation result that is harder to fix incrementally and violates the 'one logical fix at a time' principle.

## Expected Behavior

1. Before investigation dispatch (between Step 0.5 and Step 1), fix-bug should detect multi-sub-issue bugs
2. Multi-sub-issue bugs should be split into separate bug tickets (one per independent root cause or failure category)
3. Each split ticket gets its own independent investigation and fix track
4. The original compound bug is closed with a note pointing to the split tickets

## Actual Behavior

A single investigation sub-agent receives all sub-issues, produces a compound RESULT report with multiple root causes, and the fix workflow must handle N independent fixes within a single bug track.

## Scope

1. Add a 'sub-issue detection' step to fix-bug SKILL.md (between Step 0.5 and Step 1) that identifies compound bugs
2. When detected: create child bug tickets, link to parent, close parent as 'split into N sub-bugs'
3. Update bug creation guidance in create-bug/SKILL.md to require one bug per independent issue
4. Update debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode documentation to note that compound bugs should be split before processing

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — ticket describes fix-bug orchestrator behavioral failure AND fix requires editing plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (LLM-behavioral exemption): fix-bug Step 1 MUST detect compound bugs (2+ independent sub-issues) and route to cluster investigation mode. Current behavior: single investigator dispatched regardless of compound nature. Expected: sub-step 1a detects compound bugs and routes to cluster mode.
comments.author: Test
